title: quercus pagodifolia
intro: Quercus pagodifolia, also known as the Philippine white oak or pagoda oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Philippines. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Quercus pagodifolia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, making it a valuable natural remedy for various ailments. Quercus pagodifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
